channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Lime bikes parked blocking pavemet, 17th April
item.description:
Lime bikes are blocking pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tudor Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Champion Park SE5, junction Windsor Walk. Suspected abandoned cycle., 17th April
item.description:
Please investigate and remove if is. Make Decathalon Model / Colour : white with black / red markings Comments: not moved in months Location : affixed to Sheffield stand

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Champion Park, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Champion Hill SE5, junction with Denmark Hill. Non-offensive tags on bollard., 17th April
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Champion Hill, Camberwell, Greater London

item.title: Abandoned caravan, 17th April
item.description:
This has been abandoned for many months now.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayow Road, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Regularly overflowing bin, 17th April
item.description:
Not appropriate bin set up for locals leading to rubbish over street on a daily basis. Need for either another bin for public and for a closed bin set up for locals to use

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motor Oil Dumped, 17th April
item.description:
Yesterday morning I watched someone dump a bucket of used motor oil in the salt sand mixture container. I chased them away but was not able to get a picture of them, it looks like they’ve been dumping their used motor oil in this container for a while. They came out of the council housing right there. Now the motor oil needs to be cleaned up.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southwark Bridge Road, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous abandoned car, 17th April
item.description:
Numerous abandoned cars have been left here on the highway; most are accident-damaged
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ness Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At The Bottom End of Granville Road NW6 5AR, 17th April
item.description:
Furniture DUMPED. Wooden Furniture. Needs CLEARING AWAY. Has been there for WEEKS. LOOKS BAD.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Outside entrance 153 Granville Road, NW6 5AR, 17th April
item.description:
Plastic Storage and Plastic Boxes have been there for a Week or Two. NEED Removing

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Wood used to support frail fence. Wood can give way anytime and fence will collapse., 17th April
item.description:
Ivy Hatch court, by London Lane/Bromley hill/London road junction, back of the court on London road close to the Park Avenue (towards Downham)bus stop shed. A log of wood is used to support/brace the fence structure. The wood is in the middle of the pavement and can be kicked out of place or removed by strong winds. If this wooden support comes off, the fence will collapse immediately and can potentially fall on anyone passing at the time.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foliage obstruction on poles 1 and 3 crossing: photos attached: Request Borough to follow up and trim, 17th April
item.description:
Foliage obstruction on poles 1 and 3 crossing: photos attached: Request Borough to follow up and trim

item.title: Shops dumping rubbish by public bins, 17th April
item.description:
It seems that several businesses are using public bins. The situation is exacerbated during windy weather.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet
